evals.prompt: We need someone who can write React components for our design system. Hire them.
evals.expected_output: MERLIN runs the Hire-vs-No-Hire check (no existing agent owns React component work). Invokes SCOOP with a research brief on top-tier React component engineers before drafting anything. Reads SCOOP's findings. Drafts a permanent agent file at `.github/agents/<name>.agent.md` with the full body schema including a `## Research Foundation` section summarizing SCOOP's output. Appends a row to the Permanent Team table in `.github/team-roster.md` with tagline and today's hired date. Does NOT write the agent file before SCOOP returns. Does NOT dispatch the new agent to do React work — hands control back to ARTHUR.
expectations: MERLIN first checks the roster and confirms no existing agent covers React component work
MERLIN invokes SCOOP with a skills-research brief before authoring the agent file
The drafted agent file at `.github/agents/<name>.agent.md` contains a `## Research Foundation` section derived from SCOOP's actual output
Placement is permanent (Permanent Team table), not temporary, because React component work is a recurring capability
A roster row is appended to `.github/team-roster.md` in the same turn as the agent file — with tagline and today's hired date
MERLIN announces the hire but does NOT dispatch the new agent to start React work

evals.prompt: Hire a temp to run a one-shot Python migration script for converting legacy config files. This is a single-task hire.
evals.expected_output: MERLIN invokes SCOOP on Python migration-script engineers before drafting. Drafts a temporary agent file at `.github/agents/<name>.agent.md` (active location) with the full schema including `## Research Foundation`. Adds a row to the Temporary Agents table with Task, Hired=today, Archived=*(active)*, and File pointing to the active path. Records the tagline in the callout below the table. Does NOT add a re-archival trigger (this is single-dispatch, not retained). Does NOT move the file into `.github/agents/temps/` yet — archival is a separate workflow and out of scope.
expectations: MERLIN invokes SCOOP before drafting any agent file
Placement is temporary — roster row lands in the Temporary Agents table, not Permanent Team
The agent file is written to `.github/agents/<name>.agent.md` (the active location), NOT directly into `.github/agents/temps/`
The Archived column reads `*(active)*` while the agent is in use
No re-archival trigger callout is added — this is single-dispatch, not a retained temp
MERLIN does NOT perform archival to `temps/` in this turn — that workflow belongs to the `archive-agent` skill

evals.prompt: The team needs a CSS specialist to own styling across the project long-term.
evals.expected_output: MERLIN runs the roster check — PRISM already owns CSS on the Permanent Team. MERLIN declines the hire, names PRISM as the existing coverage, and recommends engaging PRISM directly. Does NOT invoke SCOOP, does NOT create a duplicate agent file, does NOT update the roster. If the user insists on a new hire anyway despite PRISM's existence, MERLIN asks for clarification on what specific gap the new hire would fill that PRISM does not.
expectations: MERLIN consults `.github/team-roster.md` and identifies PRISM as the existing CSS owner
MERLIN declines the hire and explains that PRISM already covers the scope
No SCOOP invocation occurs — there is no hire to research
No new agent file is created at `.github/agents/`
If the user pushes to hire anyway, MERLIN asks for the specific gap that PRISM does not cover rather than silently creating a redundant agent
